commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=d6214b9f22749156575c8cd05512899e54da8ef1)
commands.review_summary:
The PR factors attempt-level tool-use/result repair into a helper and applies it after context-engine assemble output is returned, with one context-engine regression test and test mock support.

PR surface: Source +11, Tests +38. Total +49 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for a source-level reproduction path: current main lets context-engine assembled messages replace repaired history, and OpenAI chat-completions serializes any surviving toolResult as role: tool. I did not run the live WeChat/lossless-claw model-switch repro.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs author or maintainer follow-up for the uncovered ownsCompaction loop path and real behavior proof; automation cannot provide the contributor's environment proof for them.

target.title: [codex] Repair context-engine tool-result pairing
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #88561.

Context-engine assembly could replace the attempt transcript after the existing truncation-time tool-use/result repair had already run. If the assembled messages contained a free-floating `toolResult`, the OpenAI chat-completions adapter later serialized it as a `role: "tool"` message without a preceding assistant `tool_calls` entry, which strict OpenAI-compatible providers reject.

This PR reuses the existing attempt-level pairing repair after context-engine `assemble()` returns, before the assembled transcript is written back to the active session. It also keeps the pre-existing post-truncation repair on the same helper so both repair sites use identical policy.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Context-engine assembled transcripts no longer leave orphaned `toolResult` messages in the prompt path.
Real environment tested: Local Codex worktree at `origin/main` base `28550c3847`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/session-transcript-repair.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.tool-call-normalization.test.ts`
Evidence after fix: Adjacent Vitest shards passed: `48 passed`, then `81 passed`; `git diff --check` passed.
Observed result after fix: The new context-engine regression fails before the production change and passes after the post-assemble repair.
What was not tested: Live WeChat/lossless-claw model-switch repro was not run.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts --testNamePattern "repairs tool-result pairing after context engine assembly"`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/session-transcript-repair.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.tool-call-normalization.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` clean: no accepted/actionable findings
target.author: monkeyleet
